# OpenShrooly v0.4.3 Release Notes

## Overview

This release tries to tidy up a lot of things that have been kind of messy in earlier releases. Better humidity control options, moves the light setting into Lux (at least for the white light), the start of a very basic on-device user interface.

## Major Changes

### Choice of Humidifier Control Mode
- **Two Control Modes Available**:
  - **Set-Point On/Off**: Simple on/off control at target humidity
  - **2% Hysteresis Band**: Turns on at target-2%, off at target (reduces cycling)
- Mode selection available through humidity settings menu
- Real-time status display shows current mode and thresholds

### Light Intensity in Lux
- **White Light Brightness Now Specified in Lux**
  - Changed from percentage/raw values to lux measurement
  - More intuitive and standardized light intensity control
  - Display shows actual lux value when lights are on

file.content:
# Agent Usage Guide

Comprehensive guide to using the agentic layer effectively.

## Table of Contents
- [Understanding the Agentic Layer](#understanding-the-agentic-layer)
- [Working with Experts](#working-with-experts)
- [Core Workflows](#core-workflows)
- [Agents](#agents)
- [Skills](#skills)
- [Meta-Agentic Commands](#meta-agentic-commands)
- [Expertise Files](#expertise-files)
- [Best Practices](#best-practices)
- [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)

---

## Understanding the Agentic Layer

The agentic layer is the outer ring around your application that enables AI agents to operate your codebase effectively.

### The Two Layers

```
┌────────────────────────────────────────┐
│           AGENTIC LAYER                │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │       APPLICATION LAYER          │  │
│  │                                  │  │
│  │  - Source code (src/)            │  │
│  │  - Database schemas              │  │
│  │  - API routes                    │  │
│  │  - Frontend components           │  │
│  │                                  │  │
│  └──────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                        │
│  - Memory files                        │
│  - Expertise (mental models)           │
│  - Skills and commands                 │
│  - 8 configured agents                │
│                                        │
└────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Key Principle: Code is Truth

The **code** is always the source of truth. Expertise files, memory files, and documentation are working mental models that help agents understand the code—but the agent should always validate against actual source files before acting.

---

## Working with Experts

Experts are domain-specific agents with maintained mental models.

### Available Experts

| Expert | Focus Area | Expertise File |
|--------|------------|----------------|
| Database | Schemas, queries, migrations | `/.agentic/expertise/database.yaml` |
| API | Endpoints, middleware, routes | `/.agentic/expertise/api.yaml` |
| Frontend | Components, state, styling | `/.agentic/expertise/frontend.yaml` |
| Pipeline | CI/CD, builds, deployment | `/.agentic/expertise/pipeline.yaml` |
| Tester | Tests, coverage, fixtures | `/.agentic/expertise/tester.yaml` |

### Using Expert Commands

#### Ask Questions
```
/experts/database-question How does order data flow through the system?
```

The expert will:
1. Load its expertise file
2. Validate knowledge against actual code
3. Provide a detailed answer with code references
4. Report confidence level

#### Trigger Self-Improvement
```
/experts/api-improve
```

The expert will:
1. Scan the codebase for relevant files
2. Compare findings against current expertise
3. Update the expertise file
4. Report changes made

### When Experts Are Most Useful

- **Before making changes**: Ask questions to understand the current state
- **After significant changes**: Run improve to update knowledge
- **When onboarding**: Use questions to explore unfamiliar code
- **For code review**: Validate understanding of changes

---

## Core Workflows

### The Prime Skill

Always start sessions with:
```
/prime
```

Or focus on a specific domain:
```
/prime database
```

This loads context (including dynamic git status and recent log) and prepares the agent for effective work.

### The Spec-Execute Cycle

#### Step 1: Spec
```
/spec Add password reset functionality
```

Creates a detailed specification in `/.agentic/specs/current/`

#### Step 2: Execute
```
/execute
```

Executes the spec with:
- Closed-loop validation (fix until it works)
- Automatic self-improvement
- Comprehensive reporting

### Team Workflow

For larger tasks, use the team orchestration:
```
/team-spec Refactor payments system
```

This creates a plan with team assignments. The orchestrator deploys builder agents (with ruff/ty validation and conventions preloaded) and validator agents (read-only). Uses TaskCreate/TaskUpdate for coordination.

### The Review Skill
```
/review
```

Reviews staged changes for:
- Code quality
- Convention adherence
- Security issues
- Test coverage

Runs in a forked context with the code-reviewer agent.

---

## Agents

8 agents configured in `.claude/agents/` with persistent memory, maxTurns, and specialized tools.

### Agent Summary

| Agent | Memory | maxTurns | Key Properties |
|-------|--------|----------|----------------|
| builder | project | 30 | skills: conventions, ruff/ty hooks, spec compliance Stop hook |
| validator | project | 20 | skills: conventions, read-only (disallows Write/Edit) |
| code-reviewer | project | 20 | plan mode, read-only tools |
| test-runner | project | 20 | can write tests |
| security-scanner | user | 20 | plan mode, read-only tools |
| meta-agent | user | 30 | creates new agents/commands |
| research-docs-fetcher | — | 20 | web scraping |
| doc-generator | project | 20 | creates documentation |

### Agent Memory

Agents with `memory: project` persist data to `.agentic/memory/` (project-scoped). Agents with `memory: user` persist to user-level storage (shared across projects).

### Builder & Validator

The **builder** and **validator** agents preload the `conventions` skill, which dynamically injects coding standards from `.agentic/memory/conventions.md`. The builder also has:

- **PostToolUse hooks** that run ruff lint and ty type-checking on every Python file written or edited
- **Stop hook** that spawns a verification agent to check the builder's work matches the assigned task/spec before allowing it to stop

Additionally, a project-level **TaskCompleted hook** (in `settings.json`) spawns an agent to verify any completed task's output against the spec. If the verification fails, the task stays incomplete and the builder gets feedback to fix the issue.

### Validation Layers

```
Write/Edit → ruff (lint) → ty (types)           ← syntax validation
TaskCompleted → agent checks work vs spec        ← semantic validation
Builder Stop → agent verifies task complete       ← completion gate
```

---

## Skills

10 skills in `.claude/skills/` using directory format with `SKILL.md` files and YAML frontmatter.

### Skills vs Commands

**Skills** are the primary interface. They support:
- Dynamic context injection (`!`command``)
- Agent delegation (`agent: code-reviewer`)
- Forked context (`context: fork`)
- Model selection (`model: opus`)
- Tool restrictions (`allowed-tools`, `disallowed-tools`)
- Hook configuration

**Commands** are utility prompts (experts, meta-agentics, deployment) that remain as flat `.md` files.

### Available Skills

| Skill | Key Properties |
|-------|----------------|
| `/prime` | Dynamic git status + log injection |
| `/spec` | `disable-model-invocation: true` |
| `/team-spec` | `model: opus`, self-validated, stop hooks |
| `/execute` | `disable-model-invocation: true` |
| `/review` | `context: fork`, `agent: code-reviewer` |
| `/run-tests` | `allowed-tools: Bash, Read` |
| `/deploy` | `disable-model-invocation: true` |
| `/start-app` | `allowed-tools: Bash, Read` |
| `conventions` | `user-invocable: false`, auto-loaded by builder/validator |

---

## Meta-Agentic Commands

Meta-agentics generate new agentic components—agents building agents.

### Create New Expert
```
/meta/create-expert websocket "WebSocket real-time events"
```

Generates:
- `/.agentic/expertise/websocket.yaml`
- `/.claude/commands/experts/websocket-question.md`
- `/.claude/commands/experts/websocket-improve.md`

### Create New Prompt
```
/meta/create-prompt analyze-deps "Analyze project dependencies"
```

Generates a new command file following established patterns.

### Create New Skill
```
/meta/create-skill docker-dev "Manage Docker development environment"
```

Generates a skill directory with `SKILL.md` and appropriate frontmatter.

---

## Expertise Files

Expertise files are YAML mental models that agents maintain.

### Structure

```yaml
domain: "database"
last_updated: "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z"
confidence_score: 0.85

key_files:
  - path: "src/db/schema/"
    purpose: "Database schema definitions"

concepts:
  - name: "User Model"
    location: "src/db/schema/user.ts"
    relationships: ["orders", "profile"]

operations:
  - name: "Run Migrations"
    command: "npm run db:migrate"

known_issues:
  - issue: "N+1 query in user list"
    location: "src/api/users.ts:67"
    severity: "medium"

recent_changes:
  - date: "2025-01-14"
    change: "Added soft delete to users"
```

### Key Sections

| Section | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `key_files` | Important files for this domain |
| `concepts` | Core concepts and relationships |
| `operations` | Common actions and how to perform them |
| `patterns` | Patterns used in this domain |
| `known_issues` | Problems and tech debt |
| `recent_changes` | Log of changes (auto-updated) |

### Confidence Score

- **0.90-1.00**: Expertise fully validated, very recent
- **0.70-0.89**: Most elements validated
- **0.50-0.69**: Partial validation, needs attention
- **Below 0.50**: Significantly stale, run improve

---

## Best Practices

### 1. Start Every Session Right
```
/prime [focus-area]
```

### 2. Spec Before Complex Work
```
/spec [description]
```
Review the spec, then `/execute`

### 3. Trust the Closed Loop
When `/execute` encounters errors, let it fix them. Only intervene if stuck after multiple attempts.

### 4. Keep Expertise Fresh
After significant work:
```
/experts/<domain>-improve
```

### 5. Create Experts for New Domains
When you start working heavily in a new area:
```
/meta/create-expert [domain] "[description]"
```

### 6. Ask Before Assuming
```
/experts/<domain>-question [question]
```
Experts provide validated, referenced answers.

### 7. Review Before Committing
```
/review
```
Catches issues before they're committed.

---

## Troubleshooting

### Expert Gives Wrong Answer

The expertise file may be stale:
```
/experts/<domain>-improve
```
Then ask again.

### Execute Keeps Failing

Check if:
1. Prerequisites are installed
2. Environment is configured
3. Tests are actually runnable

The agent will retry, but may need human input for environmental issues.

### Commands Not Found

Ensure you're in the project directory with the `.claude/` structure. Skills are in `.claude/skills/`, commands in `.claude/commands/`.

### Expertise Not Updating

Verify:
1. Files are writable
2. Codebase has files for that domain
3. Run improve command explicitly

### Low Confidence Score

Run the improve command:
```
/experts/<domain>-improve
```

If still low, the expertise file may need manual review—the domain might have changed significantly.

---

## Advanced Usage

### Multiple Experts on One Task

For critical operations, ask multiple experts:
```
/experts/database-question What tables are affected by user deletion?
/experts/api-question What endpoints handle user deletion?
/experts/tester-question What tests cover user deletion?
```

### Custom Workflows

Chain skills and commands for complex workflows:
1. `/spec` - Create specification
2. `/experts/database-question` - Validate DB approach
3. `/execute` - Implement with validation
4. `/review` - Check changes
5. `/experts/<affected>-improve` - Update expertise

### Team Workflows

For larger tasks:
1. `/team-spec` - Create team-orchestrated plan with Opus
2. `/execute` - Orchestrator deploys builder + validator agents
3. `/review` - Final review

### Scaling with Meta-Agentics

For frequently used patterns:
1. Identify the pattern
2. `/meta/create-prompt` or `/meta/create-expert`
3. Use the generated component

---

## Summary

The agentic layer enables AI agents to understand and operate your codebase effectively through:

1. **Memory Files** - Core context always available
2. **Expertise Files** - Domain-specific mental models
3. **Skills (10)** - Primary workflow interface with advanced features
4. **Commands (~19)** - Utility prompts for experts, meta-agentics, deployment
5. **Agents (8)** - Configured with persistent memory and specialized tools
6. **Self-Improvement** - Automatic knowledge updates

Start with `/prime`, use experts for informed answers, and let the closed-loop execute process handle implementation details.

tool_input.command: cat "/Users/jh/Development/Agentic engineering stuff/AgenticTemplatev2/templates/_base/"* 2>/dev/null | head -100; echo "==="; ls "/Users/jh/Development/Agentic engineering stuff/AgenticTemplatev2/templates/_base/"

tool_response.stdout:
# Project: {{PROJECT_NAME}}

> This is the main memory file for your agentic layer. It loads automatically and provides agents with immediate context.

## Quick Context

- **Language:** {{LANGUAGE}}
- **Framework:** {{FRAMEWORK}}
- **Database:** {{DATABASE_TYPE}}
- **Testing:** {{TEST_FRAMEWORK}}
- **CI/CD:** {{CICD_PLATFORM}}

## Agent Instructions

### Before Making Changes
1. Read the relevant expertise file for the domain you're working in
2. Validate your mental model against the actual source code
3. Never assume—always verify

### After Completing Work
1. Update the relevant expertise file with what changed
2. Log decisions to `/.agentic/memory/decisions.md`
3. Run validation (tests, types, lint) before reporting completion

### Source of Truth
- The **code** is always the source of truth
- Expertise files are working mental models, not documentation
- Always validate assumptions against actual files

## Key Directories

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `{{SOURCE_DIR}}` | Application code (source of truth) |
| `/.agentic/expertise` | Agent mental models by domain |
| `/.agentic/memory` | Agent memory: architecture, conventions, decisions |
| `/.agentic/specs` | Plans and specifications |
| `/.claude/skills` | Skills (directory format with SKILL.md) |
| `/.claude/agents` | Agent configurations (with memory + maxTurns) |
| `/.claude/commands` | Slash commands |

## Active Experts

| Domain | Expertise File | Question | Improve |
|--------|---------------|----------|---------|
| Database | `/.agentic/expertise/database.yaml` | `/experts/database-question` | `/experts/database-improve` |
| API | `/.agentic/expertise/api.yaml` | `/experts/api-question` | `/experts/api-improve` |
| Frontend | `/.agentic/expertise/frontend.yaml` | `/experts/frontend-question` | `/experts/frontend-improve` |
| Pipeline | `/.agentic/expertise/pipeline.yaml` | `/experts/pipeline-question` | `/experts/pipeline-improve` |
| Tester | `/.agentic/expertise/tester.yaml` | `/experts/tester-question` | `/experts/tester-improve` |

## Skills

| Skill | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `/prime` | Load context + expertise |
| `/spec` | Create implementation plan |
| `/team-spec` | Team orchestration plan (Opus, self-validated) |
| `/execute` | Closed-loop build with validation |
| `/review` | Code review (staged/unstaged/branch/file) |
| `/run-tests` | Execute project test suite |
| `/deploy` | Deploy to staging/production |
| `/start-app` | Start app in dev/production mode |
| `conventions` | Coding standards (auto-loaded by builder/validator, not user-invocable) |

## Agents

| Agent | Memory | Key Properties |
|-------|--------|----------------|
| builder | project | maxTurns: 30, skills: conventions, ruff/ty hooks |
| validator | project | maxTurns: 20, skills: conventions, read-only |
| code-reviewer | project | maxTurns: 20, plan mode, read-only tools |
| test-runner | project | maxTurns: 20, can write tests |
| security-scanner | user | maxTurns: 20, plan mode, read-only |
| meta-agent | user | maxTurns: 30, creates new agents |
| research-docs-fetcher | — | maxTurns: 20, web scraping |
| doc-generator | project | maxTurns: 20, creates documentation |

## Current Focus

<!-- Update this section to reflect current work priorities -->

**Active Work:** None

**Recent Changes:**
- Initial agentic layer setup

**Known Issues:**
- None documented yet

## Stack-Specific Notes

{{STACK_NOTES}}

## Conventions Reference

See `/.agentic/memory/conventions.md` for coding standards.

## Architecture Reference

===
CLAUDE.md.template
architecture.md.template
conventions.md.template
decisions.md.template
skills

conventions
deploy
run-tests
start-app
===
expertise
scaffold
skills
stack.yaml

# Next.js + TypeScript Stack Configuration
# Used by bootstrap and scaffold commands to customize the agentic layer

name: "nextjs-typescript"
display_name: "Next.js + TypeScript"
description: "Full-stack Next.js application with TypeScript, App Router, and modern tooling"

# Language and runtime
language: "TypeScript"
runtime: "Node.js"
framework: "Next.js"

# Package management
package_manager: "npm"
install_command: "npm install"
install_dev_command: "npm install --save-dev"

# Common commands
commands:
  dev: "npm run dev"
  build: "npm run build"
  start: "npm start"
  test: "npm run test"
  test_watch: "npm run test:watch"
  test_coverage: "npm run test:coverage"
  lint: "npm run lint"
  typecheck: "npx tsc --noEmit"
  format: "npm run format"

# Development server
dev_port: 3000

# Database options
databases:
  postgresql:
    name: "PostgreSQL"
    orm: "Prisma"
    schema_location: "prisma/schema.prisma"
    migration_cmd: "npx prisma migrate dev"
    generate_cmd: "npx prisma generate"
    seed_cmd: "npx prisma db seed"
    studio_cmd: "npx prisma studio"
  mongodb:
    name: "MongoDB"
    orm: "Prisma"
    schema_location: "prisma/schema.prisma"
    migration_cmd: "npx prisma db push"
    generate_cmd: "npx prisma generate"
  sqlite:
    name: "SQLite"
    orm: "Prisma"
    schema_location: "prisma/schema.prisma"
    migration_cmd: "npx prisma migrate dev"
    generate_cmd: "npx prisma generate"

# Testing frameworks
testing:
  jest:
    name: "Jest"
    config_file: "jest.config.js"
    test_cmd: "npm run test"
    coverage_cmd: "npm run test:coverage"
    watch_cmd: "npm run test:watch"
    test_pattern: "**/*.test.{ts,tsx}"
  vitest:
    name: "Vitest"
    config_file: "vitest.config.ts"
    test_cmd: "npm run test"
    coverage_cmd: "npm run test:coverage"
    watch_cmd: "npm run test:watch"
    test_pattern: "**/*.test.{ts,tsx}"
  playwright:
    name: "Playwright"
    config_file: "playwright.config.ts"
    test_cmd: "npx playwright test"
    test_pattern: "tests/**/*.spec.ts"

# CI/CD platforms
cicd:
  github-actions:
    name: "GitHub Actions"
    config_dir: ".github/workflows"
    node_version: "20"
  vercel:
    name: "Vercel"
    config_file: "vercel.json"

# File patterns for this stack
patterns:
  source_dir: "src/"
  api_dir: "src/app/api/"
  components_dir: "src/components/"
  hooks_dir: "src/hooks/"
  lib_dir: "src/lib/"
  types_dir: "src/types/"
  styles_dir: "src/styles/"
  public_dir: "public/"
  tests_dir: "tests/"
  tests_pattern: "**/*.test.{ts,tsx}"
  e2e_pattern: "tests/**/*.spec.ts"

# Config files
config_files:
  - "next.config.js"
  - "tailwind.config.js"
  - "tsconfig.json"
  - "postcss.config.js"
  - ".eslintrc.json"
  - ".prettierrc"

# Template variables specific to this stack
variables:
  SOURCE_DIR: "src/"
  API_DIR: "src/app/api/"
  COMPONENTS_DIR: "src/components/"
  HOOKS_DIR: "src/hooks/"
  TYPES_DIR: "src/types/"
  DEV_PORT: "3000"

# Stack-specific notes for CLAUDE.md
stack_notes: |
  ### Next.js App Router
  - API routes are in `src/app/api/`
  - Pages use file-based routing in `src/app/`
  - Server components by default, use 'use client' for client components
  - Server actions available for form handling

  ### Prisma ORM
  - Schema in `prisma/schema.prisma`
  - Run `npx prisma migrate dev` after schema changes
  - Run `npx prisma generate` to update client

  ### Styling
  - Tailwind CSS for utility-first styling
  - Global styles in `src/styles/globals.css`

# Naming conventions
naming_conventions: |
  | Element | Convention | Example |
  |---------|------------|---------|
  | Variables | camelCase | `userData`, `isActive` |
  | Functions | camelCase | `fetchUser()`, `validateInput()` |
  | Components | PascalCase | `UserCard`, `NavBar` |
  | Hooks | camelCase with use | `useUser()`, `useAuth()` |
  | Types/Interfaces | PascalCase | `User`, `ApiResponse` |
  | Constants | UPPER_SNAKE | `MAX_RETRIES`, `API_URL` |
  | Files (components) | PascalCase | `UserCard.tsx`, `NavBar.tsx` |
  | Files (utilities) | camelCase | `utils.ts`, `helpers.ts` |
  | Directories | kebab-case | `user-management/`, `api-handlers/` |

# Architecture components for diagram
architecture_components: |
  Frontend[Next.js Frontend]
  API[API Routes]
  Database[(Database)]
  Frontend --> API
  API --> Database

# Directory structure for scaffold

  ├── src/
  │   ├── app/
  │   │   ├── layout.tsx

  ├── tests/

### Device Control Flow

tool_input.old_string:
### Device Control Flow

The Shrooly device exposes a local REST API at `http://192.168.1.18`. To change settings, ShroomHub calls the device directly from the **user's browser** (same local network). The backend API only stores sensor data — it does NOT proxy device commands.

**Important:** Device control only works when the user is on the same WiFi network as the Shrooly. The UI should detect this and show a clear message when the device is unreachable.
tool_input.new_string:
### Device Control Flow

Device control works **remotely** via a command queue:

1. Dashboard sends `POST /api/commands` to api-shrooms with `{endpoint, value}`
2. Server queues the command in `device_commands` table (status: pending)
3. Shrooly polls `POST /api/commands/pending` every 60s
4. Server returns one pending command, marks as "sent"
5. Shrooly applies the setting locally

This means the dashboard works from **anywhere** — no local network required.

**16 remotely controllable endpoints:**
`target_humidity`, `humidifier__speed`, `humidity_hysteresis`, `temperature_target`, `temperature_hysteresis`, `temperature_warning_minimum`, `temperature_warning_maximum`, `air_exchange__period__min_`, `air_exchange__run_duration__s_`, `air_exchange__speed`, `lights__sunrise_hour`, `lights__duration__hours_`, `white_led_intensity`, `red_led_intensity`, `green_led_intensity`, `blue_led_intensity`

### Device Control Flow

Device control works **remotely** via a command queue:

1. Dashboard sends `POST /api/commands` to api-shrooms with `{endpoint, value}`
2. Server queues the command in `device_commands` table (status: pending)
3. Shrooly polls `POST /api/commands/pending` every 60s
4. Server returns one pending command, marks as "sent"
5. Shrooly applies the setting locally

This means the dashboard works from **anywhere** — no local network required.

**16 remotely controllable endpoints:**
`target_humidity`, `humidifier__speed`, `humidity_hysteresis`, `temperature_target`, `temperature_hysteresis`, `temperature_warning_minimum`, `temperature_warning_maximum`, `air_exchange__period__min_`, `air_exchange__run_duration__s_`, `air_exchange__speed`, `lights__sunrise_hour`, `lights__duration__hours_`, `white_led_intensity`, `red_led_intensity`, `green_led_intensity`, `blue_led_intensity`
tool_response.originalFile:
# ShroomHub - Mushroom Growing Dashboard

## Overview

A web dashboard for monitoring and controlling the OpenShrooly mushroom growing environment. View real-time sensor data, adjust growing parameters, track grows over time, and access mushroom cultivation guides.

**URL:** `shrooms.xn--datastrm-c5a.com`
**Stack:** Next.js 14 (App Router, standalone), Tailwind CSS, PostgreSQL
**Deploy:** theArchitect (Docker, port 3031)
**Data source:** `api-shrooms.xn--datastrm-c5a.com` (already deployed and receiving live data)

---

## Architecture

```
[Shrooly Device]                    [ShroomHub Frontend]
  192.168.1.18                      shrooms.datastrm.com
       |                                    |
       | POST /api/readings (every 60s)     | Server-side API routes
       v                                    v
[api-shrooms backend]  <-------- [ShroomHub API routes]
  port 3030                        port 3031
       |                              |
       v                              v
    [PostgreSQL - shrooly database]
       - sensor_readings (exists)
       - grows (new)
       - grow_logs (new)
       - grow_photos (new)
       - species_profiles (new)
```

### Device Control Flow

The Shrooly device exposes a local REST API at `http://192.168.1.18`. To change settings, ShroomHub calls the device directly from the **user's browser** (same local network). The backend API only stores sensor data — it does NOT proxy device commands.

**Important:** Device control only works when the user is on the same WiFi network as the Shrooly. The UI should detect this and show a clear message when the device is unreachable.

---

## Device API Reference (read-only from backend, control from browser)

### Sensors (GET)
| Endpoint | Returns |
|---|---|
| `/sensor/temperature` | `{id, value: 26.7, state: "26.7 °C"}` |
| `/sensor/humidity` | `{id, value: 35.3, state: "35.3 %"}` |
| `/sensor/water_level_percent` | `{id, value: 110, state: "110 %"}` |

### Controls (GET to read, POST to set via `/number/{id}/set?value=X`)
| Endpoint | Current | Unit | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `/number/target_humidity` | 70 | % | Target humidity setpoint |
| `/number/humidifier__speed` | 75 | % | Humidifier fan speed |
| `/number/temperature__minimum` | - | °C | Min temp alert threshold |
| `/number/temperature__maximum` | - | °C | Max temp alert threshold |
| `/number/air_exchange__period__min_` | 15 | min | Time between air exchanges |
| `/number/air_exchange__run_duration__s_` | 30 | s | Duration of each air exchange |
| `/number/air_exchange__speed` | 30 | % | Fan speed during exchange |
| `/number/lights__sunrise_hour` | 8 | hour | Light on time (24h) |
| `/number/lights__duration__hours_` | 12 | hours | Light duration |
| `/number/white_led_intensity` | 150 | lux | White LED brightness |
| `/number/red_led_intensity` | 0 | % | Red LED brightness |
| `/number/green_led_intensity` | 0 | % | Green LED brightness |
| `/number/blue_led_intensity` | 100 | % | Blue LED brightness |

### Real-time Events (SSE)
| Endpoint | Description |
|---|---|
| `/events` | Server-Sent Events stream, pushes `{id, value, state}` on every state change |

---

## Pages

### 1. Dashboard (`/`)

The main view. Everything at a glance.

**Live Status Cards (top row):**
- Temperature (current value, trend arrow up/down/stable vs 1h ago)
- Humidity (current value + target line)
- Water Level (visual gauge, warn if <20%)
- Fan Status (ON/OFF + RPM)
- Light Status (ON/OFF + lux value, schedule indicator)

**24h Chart (main area):**
- Line chart with temperature and humidity overlaid
- Time on X axis, dual Y axes
- Hover to see exact values at a point in time
- Toggle between 6h / 24h / 7d / 30d views
- Use the data from `GET /api/readings?hours=X`

**Quick Actions (below chart):**
- Adjust target humidity (slider)
- Adjust light schedule (sunrise hour + duration)
- These call the device API directly from the browser

**Device Status (bottom):**
- Connection status (green dot if device reachable on LAN)
- Device IP
- Last data received timestamp
- WiFi signal strength (if available)

### 2. Controls (`/controls`)

Full device configuration panel. Grouped into sections:

**Humidity Control:**
- Target humidity slider (0-100%)
- Humidifier speed slider (0-100%)
- Current humidity vs target visualization

**Temperature:**
- Min/max alert thresholds
- Current temperature display

**Air Exchange:**
- Exchange period (minutes between cycles)
- Run duration (seconds per cycle)
- Fan speed during exchange (%)

**Lighting:**
- Sunrise hour (0-23)
- Duration (hours)
- White LED intensity (lux)
- RGB LED sliders (red, green, blue 0-100%)
- Visual light schedule timeline showing on/off periods in 24h

**Each control:**
- Shows current value from device
- Slider or number input to change
- Sends POST to device on change (debounced 500ms)
- Shows success/error feedback
- Disabled state with message when device is unreachable

### 3. Grow Tracker (`/grows`)

Track individual mushroom grows from start to finish.

**Grow List:**
- Cards showing each grow (active grows first)
- Species, start date, current phase, days elapsed
- Thumbnail photo if available

**New Grow Form:**
- Species (select from species_profiles or custom text)
- Substrate type (text)
- Inoculation date
- Notes
- Auto-applies recommended settings from species profile (if selected)

**Grow Detail (`/grows/[id]`):**
- Header: species, phase, day count, status badge
- Environment chart: temp + humidity for the duration of this grow
- Phase timeline: visual bar showing spawn run -> pinning -> fruiting -> harvest
- Ability to advance phase (button to move to next phase, logs the transition)
- Daily log entries (text notes + optional photo)
- Harvest log: weight, flush number, notes
- Recommended vs actual conditions comparison (from species profile)

**Database tables needed:**

```sql
CREATE TABLE grows (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  device_id TEXT DEFAULT 'shrooly-1',
  species TEXT NOT NULL,
  substrate TEXT,
  phase TEXT DEFAULT 'spawn_run',  -- spawn_run, pinning, fruiting, harvesting, complete
  started_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  notes TEXT,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE TABLE grow_logs (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  grow_id INT REFERENCES grows(id),
  type TEXT NOT NULL,  -- note, phase_change, harvest, photo
  content TEXT,        -- note text, or phase name, or harvest weight
  metadata JSONB,      -- flexible: {from_phase, to_phase}, {weight_g, flush_number}, {photo_url}
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE TABLE grow_photos (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  grow_id INT REFERENCES grows(id),
  grow_log_id INT REFERENCES grow_logs(id),
  filename TEXT NOT NULL,
  url TEXT NOT NULL,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

### 4. Species Guide (`/guide`)

Reference library for mushroom growing conditions.

**Species Cards Grid:**
- Common species with photo/icon, name, difficulty rating
- Click to expand full profile

**Species Profile:**
- Name, description, difficulty (beginner/intermediate/advanced)
- Optimal conditions table:
  - Spawn run: temp range, humidity, duration, air exchange, light
  - Pinning: temp range, humidity, duration, air exchange, light
  - Fruiting: temp range, humidity, duration, air exchange, light
- Substrate recommendations
- Common issues & troubleshooting
- "Apply these settings" button (sends to device if on LAN)

**Seed data (species_profiles table):**

```sql
CREATE TABLE species_profiles (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  common_name TEXT,
  difficulty TEXT,  -- beginner, intermediate, advanced
  description TEXT,
  substrate_recommendations TEXT,
  troubleshooting TEXT,
  conditions JSONB NOT NULL,
  -- conditions format:
  -- {
  --   "spawn_run":  {"temp_min": 24, "temp_max": 27, "humidity": 90, "days_min": 14, "days_max": 21, "air_exchange_min": 15, "light_hours": 0},
  --   "pinning":    {"temp_min": 18, "temp_max": 22, "humidity": 95, "days_min": 5,  "days_max": 10, "air_exchange_min": 10, "light_hours": 12},
  --   "fruiting":   {"temp_min": 18, "temp_max": 24, "humidity": 85, "days_min": 5,  "days_max": 7,  "air_exchange_min": 10, "light_hours": 12}
  -- }
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

**Pre-populate with common species:**
- Oyster Mushroom (Pleurotus ostreatus) - beginner
- Shiitake (Lentinula edodes) - intermediate
- Lion's Mane (Hericium erinaceus) - intermediate
- Pink Oyster (Pleurotus djamor) - beginner
- King Oyster (Pleurotus eryngii) - intermediate
- Reishi (Ganoderma lucidum) - advanced
- Chestnut (Pholiota adiposa) - intermediate

### 5. Settings (`/settings`)

- Device IP address (configurable, default 192.168.1.18)
- Device name
- Notification preferences (water level low, temperature out of range)
- Timezone
- API key display/regenerate
- Data export (CSV download of sensor_readings)

---

## API Routes (ShroomHub backend, port 3031)

These are Next.js API routes that talk to the shared PostgreSQL database.

### Readings (proxy to existing api-shrooms data)
- `GET /api/readings/latest` — latest sensor reading
- `GET /api/readings?hours=24` — historical readings
- `GET /api/readings/stats?hours=24` — aggregated stats

### Grows
- `GET /api/grows` — list all grows
- `POST /api/grows` — create new grow
- `GET /api/grows/[id]` — grow detail with logs
- `PATCH /api/grows/[id]` — update grow (phase change, complete)
- `DELETE /api/grows/[id]` — delete grow

### Grow Logs
- `GET /api/grows/[id]/logs` — logs for a grow
- `POST /api/grows/[id]/logs` — add log entry (note, harvest, phase_change)

### Grow Photos
- `POST /api/grows/[id]/photos` — upload photo (store in /uploads or S3)
- `GET /api/photos/[filename]` — serve photo

### Species
- `GET /api/species` — list all species profiles
- `GET /api/species/[id]` — species detail

### Device (proxy for when backend is on same network)
- `GET /api/device/status` — ping device, return online/offline + current values
- Not strictly needed if browser calls device directly

---

## UI Design Notes

- Dark theme (mushrooms grow in the dark)
- Earthy color palette: deep browns, forest greens, warm amber accents
- Cards with subtle depth/shadow
- Clean sans-serif font (Inter or similar)
- Responsive: works on phone (checking the grow from the kitchen)
- Charts: use Recharts or Chart.js
- Minimal, not cluttered — the data should breathe

---

## Deployment

**Project name:** `shrooms`
**Subdomain:** `shrooms`
**Port:** 3031
**Type:** nextjs

```yaml
# project.yaml for theArchitect
name: shrooms
subdomain: shrooms
type: docker
port: 3031
description: "ShroomHub - Mushroom Growing Dashboard"
auto_ssl: true
health_check: /api/health
env:
  - DATABASE_URL=postgresql://architect:architect_dev_2026@architect-postgres:5432/shrooly
  - SHROOLY_DEVICE_IP=192.168.1.18
  - NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=https://api-shrooms.xn--datastrm-c5a.com
  - NEXT_PUBLIC_DEVICE_IP=192.168.1.18
```

---

## What Already Exists

- **api-shrooms** backend on port 3030 — receives and stores sensor data, already deployed
- **PostgreSQL** `shrooly` database with `sensor_readings` table, already has live data flowing in
- **OpenShrooly firmware** on the device, posting every 60s, controllable via local REST API
- **GitHub repo:** `Johannes0501/api-shrooms`

You need to:
1. Create a new repo (`Johannes0501/shrooms` or similar)
2. Build the Next.js app
3. Add new tables (grows, grow_logs, grow_photos, species_profiles)
4. Deploy via theArchitect

lines: -The Shrooly device exposes a local REST API at `http://192.168.1.18`. To change settings, ShroomHub calls the device directly from the **user's browser** (same local network). The backend API only stores sensor data — it does NOT proxy device commands.
+Device control works **remotely** via a command queue:
-**Important:** Device control only works when the user is on the same WiFi network as the Shrooly. The UI should detect this and show a clear message when the device is unreachable.

+2. Server queues the command in `device_commands` table (status: pending)
+4. Server returns one pending command, marks as "sent"

## Device API Reference (read-only from backend, control from browser)

tool_response.plan:
# Plan: Build ShroomHub from Spec

**Context:** ShroomHub is a greenfield Next.js 14 mushroom growing dashboard. The spec is complete at `.agentic/specs/current/SPEC.md`. No `src/` directory exists yet. The api-shrooms backend is already live, receiving device sensor data every 60s into a shared PostgreSQL database. The Shrooly device is on the LAN at 192.168.1.18. We're building on the same server that hosts theArchitect and api-shrooms.

**Deploy target:** theArchitect (auto DNS, SSL, 2FA) — push to main triggers GitHub Actions → Docker build → deploy to `shrooms.xn--datastrm-c5a.com:3031`

---

## Phase 0: Project Scaffold

Create the Next.js 14 app skeleton with all config files.

**Files:**
- `package.json` — next@14, react@18, tailwindcss, pg, recharts, typescript
- `tsconfig.json` — strict mode, `@/*` → `./src/*` path alias
- `next.config.ts` — `output: 'standalone'`, port 3031
- `tailwind.config.ts` — dark theme, earthy palette (bark #1a1208, forest #2d5016, amber #d4a017)
- `postcss.config.js` — tailwindcss + autoprefixer
- `src/app/layout.tsx` — root layout, Inter font, dark body
- `src/app/globals.css` — Tailwind directives, dark base styles
- `src/app/page.tsx` — placeholder
- `.env.local` — DATABASE_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_DEVICE_IP
- `.gitignore`, `.dockerignore`

**Validate:** `npm install && npm run dev` → page renders at localhost:3031

---

## Phase 1: Database Layer & Types

**Files:**
- `src/lib/db.ts` — pg Pool singleton (globalThis trick for dev hot reload), `query()` helper
- `src/types/database.ts` — `SensorReading`, `Grow`, `GrowLog`, `GrowPhoto`, `SpeciesProfile`, `GrowPhase`, `LogType`, `PhaseCondition`
- `src/types/device.ts` — `DeviceSensorResponse`, `DeviceNumberResponse`, `DeviceControlId`
- `src/types/api.ts` — `ApiError`, response types
- `scripts/migrate.ts` — CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS for `grows`, `grow_logs`, `grow_photos`, `species_profiles`
- `scripts/seed.ts` — INSERT 7 species profiles (Oyster, Shiitake, Lion's Mane, Pink Oyster, King Oyster, Reishi, Chestnut)
- `src/app/api/health/route.ts` — SELECT 1, returns `{ status: 'ok' }`

**Validate:** `npx tsx scripts/migrate.ts && npx tsx scripts/seed.ts && curl localhost:3031/api/health`

---

## Phase 2: API Routes

Three independent groups, buildable in parallel:

**Group A — Readings:**
- `src/app/api/readings/latest/route.ts` — latest sensor_reading
- `src/app/api/readings/route.ts` — historical readings with `?hours=24&limit=1000`
- `src/app/api/readings/stats/route.ts` — aggregated stats

**Group B — Grows CRUD:**
- `src/app/api/grows/route.ts` — GET list, POST create
- `src/app/api/grows/[id]/route.ts` — GET detail, PATCH update, DELETE
- `src/app/api/grows/[id]/logs/route.ts` — GET logs, POST add log

**Group C — Species + Device:**
- `src/app/api/species/route.ts` — GET list
- `src/app/api/species/[id]/route.ts` — GET detail
- `src/app/api/device/status/route.ts` — ping device, return online/offline

**Validate:** curl each endpoint, verify JSON responses

---

## Phase 3: Layout, Hooks & Shared Components

**Layout:**
- `src/components/layout/Sidebar.tsx` — nav links (Dashboard, Controls, Grows, Guide, Settings), active highlighting, collapses to bottom bar on mobile
- `src/components/layout/Header.tsx` — "ShroomHub" title, device status dot
- Update `src/app/layout.tsx` — integrate Sidebar + Header

**Hooks:**
- `src/hooks/useDevice.ts` — reachability check (30s poll), `fetchControl(id)`, `setControl(id, value)`, AbortController cleanup
- `src/hooks/useSensorData.ts` — SSE from device `/events`, fallback to polling `/api/readings/latest` every 60s
- `src/hooks/useReadingsHistory.ts` — fetch `/api/readings?hours=X`

**UI Components:**
- `src/components/ui/SensorCard.tsx` — icon, label, value, unit, trend, warning state
- `src/components/ui/SliderControl.tsx` — debounced 500ms, disabled when offline
- `src/components/ui/StatusBadge.tsx` — online/offline/active/complete variants
- `src/components/ui/Chart.tsx` — Recharts LineChart wrapper, dual Y-axis, dark theme
- `src/components/ui/PhaseTimeline.tsx` — horizontal phase bar
- `src/components/ui/LoadingSpinner.tsx`
- `src/components/ui/EmptyState.tsx`

**Constants:**
- `src/lib/deviceControls.ts` — control ID → endpoint mapping, min/max/step/unit for each control

**Validate:** navigate between pages, sidebar works, dark theme visible, mobile responsive

---

## Phase 4: Dashboard & Controls Pages

**Dashboard (`/`):**
- `src/app/page.tsx` — client component, uses useSensorData + useReadingsHistory
- `src/components/dashboard/SensorCardsRow.tsx` — 5 cards: temp, humidity, water, fan, light
- `src/components/dashboard/EnvironmentChart.tsx` — line chart with 6h/24h/7d/30d selector
- `src/components/dashboard/QuickActions.tsx` — humidity slider + light schedule
- `src/components/dashboard/DeviceStatus.tsx` — connection dot, IP, last update

**Controls (`/controls`):**
- `src/app/controls/page.tsx` — organized into collapsible sections
- `src/components/controls/HumiditySection.tsx` — target + speed sliders
- `src/components/controls/TemperatureSection.tsx` — min/max thresholds
- `src/components/controls/AirExchangeSection.tsx` — period, duration, speed
- `src/components/controls/LightingSection.tsx` — sunrise, duration, white LED, RGB sliders, visual timeline

**Validate:** live sensor values display, chart renders historical data, control sliders send POST to device

---

## Phase 5: Grow Tracker

**Files:**
- `src/app/grows/page.tsx` — grow list with cards, "New Grow" button
- `src/components/grows/GrowCard.tsx` — species, phase badge, days elapsed
- `src/components/grows/NewGrowForm.tsx` — species select, substrate, notes, date
- `src/app/grows/[id]/page.tsx` — grow detail
- `src/components/grows/GrowDetail.tsx` — header, phase timeline, chart, logs
- `src/components/grows/GrowLogEntry.tsx` — renders note/phase_change/harvest differently
- `src/components/grows/AddLogForm.tsx` — type selector, content, harvest fields
- `src/components/grows/PhaseControl.tsx` — advance phase button
- `src/components/grows/ConditionsComparison.tsx` — recommended vs actual

**Validate:** create grow, add logs, advance phases, verify detail page

---

## Phase 6: Species Guide & Settings

**Guide (`/guide`):**
- `src/app/guide/page.tsx` — species card grid
- `src/components/guide/SpeciesCard.tsx` — name, difficulty badge, description
- `src/components/guide/SpeciesDetail.tsx` — expanded profile
- `src/components/guide/ConditionsTable.tsx` — phase conditions table
- `src/components/guide/ApplySettingsButton.tsx` — push conditions to device

**Settings (`/settings`):**
- `src/app/settings/page.tsx` — config panels
- `src/components/settings/DeviceConfig.tsx` — IP input, test connection
- `src/components/settings/DataExport.tsx` — CSV download
- `src/components/settings/NotificationPrefs.tsx` — browser notifications (v1: localStorage)

**Validate:** species render with conditions, apply settings works, CSV export downloads

---

## Phase 7: Docker & Deployment

**Files:**
- `Dockerfile` — multi-stage: deps → build standalone → runner (node:20-alpine, port 3031, healthcheck)
- `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` — triggered by theArchitect webhook on push to main
- `src/app/not-found.tsx`, `src/app/error.tsx`, `src/app/loading.tsx`

**Deployment via theArchitect:**
1. `project.yaml` already exists in repo root with correct config
2. Register project via theArchitect API or dashboard (creates GitHub repo + webhook)
3. Push code to main → webhook triggers GitHub Actions → Docker build → deploy
4. theArchitect auto-handles: DNS (shrooms.xn--datastrm-c5a.com via Namecheap), SSL (certbot), nginx proxy (port 3031), health check
5. Container joins `architect-net` Docker network, accesses PostgreSQL at `architect-postgres:5432`

**DATABASE_URL note:** In Docker on architect-net, use `postgresql://architect:architect_dev_2026@architect-postgres:5432/shrooly` (container name). The `project.yaml` env already has this correct.

**Validate:** `docker build -t shroomhub . && docker run -p 3031:3031 ...` → health check passes, all pages render

---

## Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|-----------|
| Device control endpoint IDs may differ from spec | Verify against actual device early in Phase 4; centralize mapping in `deviceControls.ts` |
| SSE connection drops | Reconnect with backoff, fall back to polling |
| Migration affects shared DB | Only CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, never ALTER existing tables |
| CORS on device calls from browser | ESPHome allows `*` by default; verify early |

---

## Total: ~73 files across 8 phases

Build order is sequential by phase, but within each phase many files are independent and can be built in parallel.
